question_text: A roller coaster at the top of a hill has a large gravitational potential energy store. As it descends the hill, what happens to this energy?

options.label: B
options.text: It is transferred to kinetic energy and thermal energy stores

options.label: C
options.text: It is converted to chemical energy

options.label: D
options.text: It remains in the gravitational potential energy store

correct_answer: It is transferred to kinetic energy and thermal energy stores
explanation: As the roller coaster descends the hill, gravitational potential energy is converted into kinetic energy (which makes the roller coaster move faster) and thermal energy (produced by friction between the track and the coaster, and air resistance). Energy is not destroyed; it is transferred from one store to another.
